title: Elementary administration occupations n.e.c.
description: Photograph people, landscapes, merchandise, or other subjects. May use lighting equipment to enhance a subject's appearance. May use editing software to produce finished images and prints. Includes commercial and industrial photographers, scientific photographers, and photojournalists. Handle baggage for travelers at transportation terminals or for guests at hotels or similar establishments. Compile information and records to draw up purchase orders for procurement of materials and services. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Perform maintenance tasks necessary to keep equipment working properly.
File, store, and clean master copies or plates, and maintain stock of supplies according to classification.
Select materials needed, set up and adjust machines, and load them with required media to complete work assignments.
Read job orders to determine work specifications and verify required quantities, materials, and production needs.
Operate and monitor office and auxiliary machines to ensure proper operation during processes.
Sort, assemble, proof, and complete records of production, including work volumes and materials used.
Eliminate outdated materials and maintain files to ensure accuracy and compliance with guidelines.
Direct activities and engage workers in the setup and operation of photographic equipment and processes.
Compute prices, request and maintain inventories, and manage purchasing files and documentation for supplies.
Transfer, enhance, and manipulate photographs, and use various photographic techniques for quality assurance.
Store completed documents and sensitive materials in appropriate locations for easy retrieval and archiving.
Communicate with suppliers and customers regarding orders, delivery statuses, and any product-related inquiries.
Assist guests with inquiries, transportation arrangements, and provide relevant information about services.
Monitor material inventory levels, arrange delivery and assembly of supplies, and requisition items as needed.
Maintain clean and organized environments for travelers and guests, encompassing lobbies and public areas.
Collect and manage financial transactions, maintaining accurate records and processing payments as necessary.
Engage in research to develop new photographic procedures and employ specialized techniques to enhance work quality.
Maintain knowledge of all organizational and governmental rules affecting purchases and provide information about these rules to staff and vendors.
Monitor in-house inventory movement and complete inventory transfer forms for bookkeeping purposes.
Communicate with customers, employees, and other individuals to answer questions, disseminate information, take orders, and address complaints.
Open, sort, and route incoming mail, answer correspondence, and prepare outgoing mail.
Answer telephones, direct calls, and take messages.
Compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities, business transactions, and other activities.
Complete and mail bills, contracts, policies, invoices, or checks.
Operate office machines, such as photocopiers and scanners, facsimile machines, voice mail systems, and personal computers.
Type, format, proofread, and edit correspondence and other documents, from notes or dictating machines, using computers or typewriters.
Prepare purchase orders and send copies to suppliers and departments originating requests.
Prepare documents and correspondence, such as damage claims, credit and billing inquiries, invoices, and service complaints.
Gather records pertinent to specific problems, review them for completeness and accuracy, and attach records to correspondence as necessary.
Maintain and update filing, inventory, mailing, and database systems, either manually or using a computer.
Review files, records, and other documents to obtain information to respond to requests.
Notify departments when supplies of specific items are low, or when orders would deplete available supplies.
Verify customer and order information for correctness, checking it against previously obtained information as necessary.
Compose letters in reply to correspondence concerning requests for merchandise, damage claims, or billing issues.
